svg.text: PHASE 4 — DUAL REDUNDANT OBSTACLE AVOIDANCE ARRAYS
© 2026 Steve Griffing PE(CSE) CISSP-ISSEP CPP · CC BY 4.0
SENSOR PLACEMENT — TOP VIEW (18" hull, not to scale)
● Array A (Node 3, solid) · ○ Array B (Node 2, hollow) — fully independent buses
● Array A (solid) · ○ Array B (hollow dashed) — axial offset provides spatial diversity
5mm PMMA disc · 0.5mm · UV-cure · sensor on 0.3mm PETG shoulder
Two fully independent buses — each node covers all 6 axes if the other fails
Node 2 covers all axes on Node 3 fail
Node 3 covers all axes on Node 2 fail
Same I²C addresses (0x70 / 0x20 / 0x54–0x59) appear on both arrays — address collision is impossible because the buses are electrically isolated on separate CM3-CARRIER-1 nodes.
Array A (Panel D) then Array B (Panel B)
Cut 12× PMMA discs 5mm dia × 0.5mm from clear acrylic.
Mark Array A: S1A@25mm, S2A@440mm, S3A/S4A@200mm,
Mark Array B: S1B@40mm, S2B@425mm, S3B/S4B@150mm,
Drill 6.5mm at all 12 positions. Deburr from inside.
Press-fit mounts. Solder VL53L5CX on 12 breakout PCBs.
Seat PCBs. UV-adhesive PMMA discs over apertures. Cure 60s.
Route Array A cables → Panel D via separate conduit.
Route Array B cables → Panel B via separate conduit.
Assemble 2× TCA9548A+MCP23008 PCBs. Connect Array A
to Node 3 I²C3; Array B to Node 2 I²C2.
Flash both nodes: vl53l5cx_mux firmware. Verify
Failover test: disable Node 3 — verify Array B covers
all 6 axes. Then swap — Node 2 off, Array A holds.
⚠ Keep all 12 PMMA apertures clear of paint, foam and tape. Do NOT merge Arrays A and B onto the same I²C bus — this defeats the no-SPOF design. Route harnesses through separate conduits.
VL53L5CX VCSEL is Class 1 eye-safe. Condensation inside aperture = seal failure — re-apply UV adhesive.
COVERAGE REDUNDANCY — 65° FoV (±32.5°) per sensor · 4.0m range indoor
Sensor: ST VL53L5CX · 8×8 multizone ToF · 65° diagonal FoV · 0.4–4.0m indoor · 15 Hz · I²C 1 MHz · 6.4×3.0×1.5mm · Class 1 VCSEL · $7 ea
Arrays: 12 sensors total (2×6) · Array A on Node 3 I²C3 (Panel D) · Array B on Node 2 I²C2 (Panel B) · Independent TCA9548A (0x70) + MCP23008 (0x20) per array · +52g total
Fuselage EDF: AEORC XDF40 40mm 8-blade 6500KV 41g · 575g nominal / 698g peak @ 4S · powered via cells 1–4 balance tap · 50A 4S ESC · no 6S motor swap exists for 40mm EDFs
© 2026 Steve Griffing, PE(CSE) CISSP-ISSEP CPP · CC BY 4.0 · Visual inspiration: Firefly/Serenity © Joss Whedon/Mutant Enemy/Universal — Fan engineering
